#hiring Next.js Web Developer

Apply here: https://lnkd.in/g_UiR4Ed

PureSoft Labs OU, a software company based in Estonia, is looking for a developer with strong web development experience, especially with React and Next.js, and a solid ability to use AI tools to work faster and more effectively.We want someone who can take an app idea, work with the APIs and databases we provide, and build the product from there. The role also includes ongoing updates, refinements, and maintenance as needed.What we are looking for:Strong experience with modern web developmentComfortable using AI code editors and other AI tools to accelerate developmentAble to quickly set up web apps and then refine them to a high standardFocus on clean, high-quality workAble to work independently and communicate clearlyCompensation: depending on skills, experience, and quality of work. Final rate will be discussed before the contract is finalized.This is intended as a long-term contract opportunity.There might be a short technical task as part of the process, but it is only for assessment purposes. It will be small, self-contained, and will not require access to any source code. We are not looking for free work.

🌐 Opportunity: Hindi Content Annotation Specialist at Mercor
Mercor is hiring Hindi-speaking Content Annotation Specialists for a high-impact project focused on content integrity and trust & safety in AI systems.
🔹 About the Role
You’ll review short-form Hindi video content and produce structured evaluations that help train advanced AI models. This role requires strong analytical skills, cultural awareness, and the ability to articulate nuanced insights in both Hindi and English.
🔹 Responsibilities
Review and evaluate Hindi-language video content.
Write detailed assessments in English, capturing nuance, subtext, and cultural context.
Identify problematic content (coded language, suggestive framing, cultural subtext).
Apply structured rating frameworks for content appropriateness.
Research slang, memes, and regional references to ensure accuracy.
Triage content that doesn’t meet review criteria.
⚠️ Content Warning: This role involves exposure to disturbing or harmful content (violence, explicit language, mature themes). A waiver is required before onboarding.
🔹 Requirements
Native/fluent Hindi speaker with deep knowledge of internet culture, slang, and memes.
Professional writing ability in Hindi and English.
Strong analytical and interpretive skills.
Ability to evaluate sensitive content objectively.
Familiarity with short-form social media video formats.
Reliable internet connection.
🔹 Ideal Candidate
Experience in content annotation, moderation, or trust & safety.
Comfortable working with sensitive content daily.
Detail-oriented with strong written communication skills.
Able to recognize cultural nuances that shift meaning.
🔹 Contract & Payment
Hourly contract: $32–$40/hour
Fully remote, flexible schedule
Weekly payments via Stripe or Wise
Independent contractor engagement
🔹 About Mercor
Mercor partners with leading AI labs and enterprises to train frontier models using human expertise. You’ll gain hands-on exposure to real-world AI research and contribute directly to shaping the next generation of AI systems.

Position Overview:

We are looking for a passionate and motivated Junior Web Developer with at least 1 year of hands-on experience in web development. The ideal candidate should have a strong foundation in PHP, JavaScript, and CSS, and be eager to learn and grow in a fast-paced development environment.

Key Responsibilities:
Develop, test, and maintain dynamic web applications using PHP
Write clean, efficient, and reusable code
Collaborate with designers and senior developers to implement UI/UX features
Work with front-end technologies including HTML, CSS, and JavaScript
Troubleshoot, debug, and upgrade existing systems
Integrate APIs and third-party services
Ensure responsive design and cross-browser compatibility

Requirements:
Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, IT, or related field (or equivalent experience)
1+ year of professional or freelance development experience
Strong knowledge of PHP
Good understanding of JavaScript (ES6 preferred)
Proficiency in HTML5 & CSS3
Basic knowledge of databases (MySQL or similar)
Understanding of version control systems (e.g., Git)
Familiarity with responsive design principles
Problem-solving and analytical thinking

Preferred Skills: (Optional)
Experience with PHP frameworks (e.g., Laravel)
Knowledge of frontend libraries (e.g., React, Vue.js)
Basic understanding of RESTful APIs
Familiarity with Bootstrap or Tailwind CSS

Sitting in a Costa Coffee in DIFC staring at a signed 40,000 AED/month contract, I almost made a massive mistake.

My first thought was to immediately hire two more media buyers to handle the workload. That meant 16,000 AED in monthly salaries, two more visas, and upgrading our Business Bay office space.

My brain defaulted to the old agency model: more revenue equals more headcount.

But scaling your overhead at the exact same rate as your revenue isn't actually scaling. It's just getting heavier.

We paused the hiring. Instead, we spent four days building custom AI scripts to automate our campaign reporting and ad-testing workflows.

The new operational cost? About 750 AED a month in API credits.

We serviced the client with our existing team at PyramediaX, delivered the campaign early, and actually kept our profit margin intact. Throwing bodies at a growing business is the easiest way to bleed cash.

What's the most expensive scaling mistake you've ever made?

#DigitalMarketing #ContentStrategy #DubaiBusiness

My first thought was to immediately hire two more media buyers to handle the workload. That meant 16,000 AED in monthly salaries, two more visas, and upgrading our Business Bay office space.

But scaling your overhead at the exact same rate as your revenue isn't actually scaling. It's just getting heavier.

We paused the hiring. Instead, we spent four days building custom AI scripts to automate our campaign reporting and ad-testing workflows.

The new operational cost? About 750 AED a month in API credits.

We serviced the client with our existing team at PyramediaX, delivered the campaign early, and actually kept our profit margin intact. Throwing bodies at a growing business is the easiest way to bleed cash.

#hiring CI/CD Engineer

Apply here: https://lnkd.in/gAhq7kvn

Als CI/CD Engineer bij Conclusion Xforce werk je aan het bouwen en beheren van moderne gestandaardiseerde deployment pipelines in enterprise omgevingen. Je verbetert de snelheid en betrouwbaarheid van softwarelevering en integreert security- en compliance tooling bij klanten zoals Schiphol, NS en Rabobank.  Jouw rol In deze rol bouw en beheer je herbruikbare en schaalbare CI/CD pipelines binnen complexe omgevingen. Je optimaliseert bestaande workflows voor snelheid betrouwbaarheid en veiligheid en werkt met tooling zoals Azure DevOps GitLab GitHub Actions of Jenkins. Je integreert security- en compliance controles zoals SAST DAST en dependency scans en implementeert secrets-management met tools zoals HashiCorp Vault en Azure Key Vault. Je ondersteunt development teams met adoptie van CI/CD best practices implementeert GitOps workflows en artifact management en helpt bij troubleshooting en performance optimalisatie. Je werkt hands-on maar houdt ook het overzicht en draagt bij aan kennisdeling binnen het team. Je bent onderdeel van een no-nonsense cultuur waarin samenwerking met collegas belangrijk is en je oplossingen hebt die echt waarde toevoegen voor de klant. The legal industry is undergoing a massive transformation—and those who adapt now will access some of the highest-paying global opportunities.
Here’s what’s changing 👇
🌍 1. Cross-Border Compliance is the New Goldmine
With regulations tightening worldwide (data, ESG, AI), companies are actively hiring lawyers who can navigate multi-jurisdictional frameworks.
Think beyond domestic law—global advisory is where the money is.
🤖 2. AI Regulation is Creating Premium Legal Roles
From the EU AI Act to evolving policies in the United States and India, organizations need legal experts who understand:
AI governance
Data liability
Algorithmic accountability
💰 These roles are already commanding $120K–$300K globally.
🏦 3. Regulatory Bodies & FinTech Are Hiring Aggressively
Institutions like Securities and Exchange Board of India and Reserve Bank of India are expanding compliance frameworks—while global fintechs need legal minds who can bridge regulation + innovation.
📑 4. Contract & Risk Specialists Are in Demand Worldwide
If you have experience in:
✔️ Cross-border contracts
✔️ Compliance frameworks
✔️ Risk mitigation

✨ My Take:
The future of law is no longer local—it’s strategic, tech-driven, and global.
Lawyers who position themselves at the intersection of compliance + technology + international law will dominate the next decade.

Global Legal Consulting

Cross-Border Contract Management
If you’re hiring or know someone who is, let’s connect.

Brutal truth. No filters.
If you’re on LinkedIn hunting for BPO projects  especially inbound, customer care, support, or non-voice ,

you’re not an entrepreneur. You’re a target.

Inbound is NOT “available in the market” the way it’s being sold to you. That era is gone. Automation, AI, and self-service platforms have already wiped out a massive portion of that demand. The little that remains? It’s tightly controlled by established vendors who have spent YEARS building trust, metrics, and relationships.

Real clients don’t “post requirements.”
Real clients don’t “entertain new vendors in comments.”
Real clients don’t “offer fixed per seat income to strangers.”
They already have vendors.
They already have SLAs.
They already have escalation chains.
And most importantly - they already know who you are NOT.

So who’s left on your feed?
Scammers.
Process resellers with zero authority.
Middlemen stacking commissions on imaginary deals.
And content designed to bait beginners with words like:
“Fixed payout”
“Assured seats”
“Immediate onboarding”
“No sales needed”
Wake up that’s not business. That’s bait.

And the most dangerous part?
The illusion of legitimacy.
You’ll be shown:
Professional-looking agreements
Company profiles
Email trails
“Client calls”
Even video meetings
Everything will look perfect - until your money is gone.

Because this is no longer small-time fraud.

This is organized, layered, well-practiced deception targeting people who are desperate to “start something with fix income ”

Now let’s talk about YOU.

Most new BPO owners fail not because they lack effort but because they lack understanding of how deals actually happen.
You know hiring.
You know operations.
You know shifts and KPIs.
That’s HALF knowledge.

You don’t know:
Client acquisition as a business owner and investor . ( Job in some mncs dont count in this ) . 
Contract negotiation
Risk ownership
Revenue structuring
Network-based entry
And without that  you’re not running a business.

You’re just preparing infrastructure for someone else’s promise.
And then comes the biggest delusion:

“I have 50 seats, good office, ready to start.”
No one cares.
Let me repeat - NO ONE CARES.
Clients don’t pay for your chairs, your interiors, or your location.

They pay for:
Proven results
Consistency under pressure
Data security
Process maturity
And TRUST built over time
And trust does NOT come from brokers or LinkedIn comments.

It comes from reputation and references.
So here’s the only real path  whether you like it or not:

If you want inbound someday, earn it.
Grind in outbound.
Face rejections.
Learn how to bring revenue.
Build case studies.
Create real references.
Stay in the game long enough to be noticed.
Because inbound is not given.
It is handed over  only when you’re already proven.

#BPOProjects #Outsourcing #CallCenter #InboundProcess #OutboundProcess 
#LeadGeneration #CustomerSupport #BusinessOpportunity 
#NoUpfrontFee #StartupIndia #Operations

Something happened recently that I want to share with all of you.

One of the participants from my AI Coding Assistant training session reached out to me last week with some great news. He cleared all four rounds of interviews at a well-known firm and got placed in a contract role. For him, this was a dream company, and he made it happen.

Now here is the interesting part that made me think and write this post.

During one of the interview rounds, they gave him a scenario and asked him to create diagrams. He confidently said he could do it using AI coding assistant tools. The interviewers did not stop him. In fact, they encouraged him to go ahead and use them. But before he hit enter on his prompt, they asked him something powerful. They asked him to explain what kind of diagram it would generate, what components would be part of it, and how the flow would look. He explained everything clearly, and then he generated the diagram.

They appreciated two things. First, the capability of the tool itself. Second, and more importantly, the candidate's ability to visualize and articulate the output before the tool even produced it. That distinction matters more than people realize.

The remaining rounds covered areas like Markdown, Spec Driven development, system design and architecture. He cleared all four rounds because he was solid in his fundamentals and knew how to leverage modern tools effectively. Both worked together.

But the bigger message here is this. The interview landscape is changing. Companies are no longer just asking you to recall syntax from memory or testing whether you can write something on a whiteboard without any reference. They are now evaluating how well you understand systems, how effectively you can leverage AI-powered tools, and most importantly, whether you truly understand what those tools are doing for you.

If you are someone who is hiring, consider adopting this approach. Test for understanding, not just memorization. If you are someone who is preparing for interviews, take note of this shift. Sharpen your fundamentals, learn to work with modern AI tools, and focus on building the ability to think before you prompt.

And if you ever come across a company that interviews like this, do not let that opportunity go. These are the organizations that are building for the future. They value capability over ceremony, and understanding over recitation.

The world is moving forward. Make sure your preparation is moving with it.
